description_long: This is a tall, slender stone obelisk with a weathered, rectangular shaft that tapers slightly as it rises. The surface is mottled with gray, white, and patches of yellowish lichen, indicating age and exposure to the elements. The base is a multi-tiered, square plinth, also made of stone, with visible moss and organic debris around its perimeter. One face of the obelisk features faint, vertically aligned inscriptions, while the other sides are mostly plain. The tip of the obelisk is pointed and slightly chipped, adding to its aged appearance. The monument is set on a rough, stone platform surrounded by patches of grass and moss.
description: A weathered stone obelisk with a pointed tip, set on a multi-tiered square base and surrounded by moss and grass. One face features faint inscriptions, while the rest are plain and aged.
description_view_0: Front view showing the obelisk's pointed tip, mottled stone surface, and a clear view of the multi-tiered base with moss around it.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the obelisk's tapering shaft and the layered stone base, with visible yellowish lichen and moss.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, weathered stone shaft and the square base, with more visible moss and organic debris.
description_view_3: View showing the inscribed face of the obelisk, with vertical text and a clear look at the aged, mossy base.
attribute_inscription: One face (view 3) has faint, vertically aligned inscriptions that are weathered and difficult to read; the style appears to be engraved text, but the language and full content are not legible.
attribute_base_design: The base is a multi-tiered, square stone plinth with visible weathering, moss, and organic debris. It consists of several stacked rectangular layers, each slightly smaller than the one below, forming a stepped appearance.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is pointed and slightly chipped, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features; it maintains a simple, classic obelisk form.
attribute_surrounding_features: The obelisk is surrounded by patches of moss, grass, and organic debris, with no other notable objects or structures immediately adjacent.
